# File Hash via Streaming

Implement a file content hasher that computes a hash digest by processing data as a stream, using the hash object as a Transform stream.

## Capabilities

### Hash data using stream piping

Hash a Buffer or Readable stream of data by piping it through a hash stream, collecting the digest on stream end.

- Piping a Buffer containing `"hello world"` through a SHA-256 hash stream produces the hex digest `"b94d27b9934d3e08a52e52d7da7dabfac484efe37a5380ee9088f7ace2efcde9"` [@test](./tests/stream-basic.test.js)
- Piping a Buffer containing `""` (empty) through a SHA-256 hash stream produces `"e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855"` [@test](./tests/stream-empty.test.js)
- The streaming result matches the non-streaming hash result for the same input data [@test](./tests/stream-vs-direct.test.js)
- The hash stream can process data in multiple chunks via multiple write() calls before ending, producing the correct combined digest [@test](./tests/stream-chunks.test.js)

## Implementation

[@generates](./src/index.js)

## API

```javascript { #api }
/**
 * Hash data by piping it through a hash Transform stream.
 * @param {Buffer|string} data - Data to hash
 * @param {string} [algorithm="sha256"] - Hash algorithm
 * @returns {Promise<string>} Resolves to the hex digest string
 */
export function hashViaStream(data, algorithm = "sha256");
```

## Dependencies { .dependencies }

### crypto-browserify { .dependency }

Provides browser-compatible implementation of Node.js's `crypto` module, including createHash which returns a Transform stream.

[@satisfied-by](crypto-browserify)
